intro: Deyeuxia robusta, also known as robust Deyeuxia or Chinese beard grass, is a species of grass native to China and other parts of East Asia, characterized by its tall stems and feathery inflorescences. Traditionally, Deyeuxia robusta has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Deyeuxia robusta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Deyeuxia robusta is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and it may also be formulated into capsules or tablets for modern use.
